questions.question: Consider a repeated Prisoner's Dilemma game where two prisoners have the option to either confess or remain silent. If both prisoners confess, they each receive a 2-year sentence. If one confesses and the other remains silent, the confessor receives a 1-year sentence and the silent prisoner receives a 0-year sentence. If both remain silent, they each receive a 1-year sentence. Determine the optimal strategy for the prisoners in the long run. (5 marks)

A firm has a repeated partnership game with two other firms. In the first stage of the game, the firms agree on a profit-sharing ratio. In the subsequent stages, the firms engage in a simultaneous game where they decide on production levels. Determine the optimal strategy for the firms, assuming that they aim to maximize their profits in the long run. (10 marks)

Consider a repeated game of Chicken, where two drivers try to outmaneuver each other. If both drivers maintain their speed, they both receive a 1-point penalty. If one driver backs down, the other driver receives a 3-point penalty. Determine the optimal strategy for the drivers in the long run, assuming that they aim to minimize their penalties. (5 marks)
